decision: Presentation-mode masking stays PATTERN-BASED on the client; do NOT wire knownValues into the browser masker
context: issue #155 Copilot comments on Terminal.tsx, GhosttyTerminal.tsx, TerminalPlayer.tsx flagged createStreamMasker() being called without knownValues
rationale: Investigation confirmed the app's known secret values (lib/secrets.ts server-file store; api-tools credentials store) exist ONLY server-side. Their APIs (app/api/secrets, app/api/api-tools/credentials) return masked values, never raw ones. Shipping raw secret values to the browser to enable exact-value masking would be a security regression. mask.ts still SUPPORTS knownValues for a future server-side redact path; the client deliberately relies on secret-shape patterns only. Recorded scope + added explanatory comments at all three call sites.

decision: Flush the stream masker before raw writes when presentation mode is OFF in TerminalPlayer.writeChunk
context: plugins/terminal-recorder/components/TerminalPlayer.tsx raw-write branch wrote data without flushing masker carry
rationale: If masking was on immediately before, a partial token/escape buffered in the masker would be lost or emitted late at the mode-off transition. Fix mirrors the already-correct live Terminal.tsx / GhosttyTerminal.tsx write paths (flush carried bytes, then write raw). Masker-level contract covered by mask.test.ts OFF->ON toggle test.
